response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
I've been following this whole social media thing from the sidelines, you know? I work as a waiter in Los Angeles, but I don't use any of these platforms myself. My husband works there too, so we're kind of immersed in it all day long. Anyway, I'm not depressed anymore because my daughter just moved into our home and she keeps me busy with cooking and rock climbing, which are some of my favorite hobbies now.

Back to the fake content on social media: well, when people say they're dealing with it, honestly it sounds like a lot of work for someone like me who's not really tech-savvy. I mean, at 57 years old, you get used to things but learning new stuff is tough.

I think the best way might be to just keep scrolling until something positive catches your eye – like when we watch movies together or go climbing. The bad stuff just fades away if you ignore it enough.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary:
I considered my own background as a non-tech user and how depression affected my perspective, then applied that to social media fake content by focusing on avoidance strategies similar to ignoring negative things in life.

I thought about the persona traits – age moderate, low conscientiousness which means relying on simple routines, moderate neuroticism so preferring less stress methods.
